question: Write C++ functions for matrix operations including AllocateMemory, InitializeMatrix, DisplayMatrix, DeallocateMemory, sumCol (column sums), and diag (diagonal elements). Use dynamic memory allocation with new/delete operators.

test_cases.expected:
The array is:
0 0 0 
0 0 0 
Column sums: 0 0 0 
Diagonal elements: 0 0

The array is:
0 0 0 
0 0 0 
0 0 0 
Column sums: 0 0 0 
Diagonal elements: 0 0 0

The array is:
0 0 0 0 
Column sums: 0 0 0 0 
Diagonal elements: 0

errors: Poor function names (f1, f2, f3, f4, f5, f6) instead of descriptive names
Single letter variable names (r, c, m, s, d, x) reducing readability
Poor code formatting and inconsistent indentation
No comments explaining function purposes or complex logic
Inefficient use of endl instead of \n for better performance
Poor cache locality in column sum calculation
Redundant calculations and inefficient nested loops
Multiple statements on single line reducing readability
Missing proper code organization and structure
